title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:1
body: When earth is shaken when it is rocked for the rising of the Hour with its final quake with its most violent rocking one that befits its magnitude

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:2
body: and the earth brings forth its burdens its hidden treasures and its dead casting them onto its surface

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:3
body: and man the disbeliever in resurrection says ‘What is wrong with it?’ — in denial of that situation.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:4
body: On that day yawma’idhin substitutes for and is the response to idhā ‘when’ it shall relate its chronicles it shall inform of the good and evil deeds committed in it

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:5
body: for its Lord will have inspired it that is He will have commanded her to do this — in a hadīth it is stated ‘It the earth shall testify to every single deed committed by every servant and handmaiden upon its surface’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:6
body: On that day mankind shall issue forth they will depart from the site of the Reckoning in separate groups divided up so that those taking it their book by the right hand will go to Paradise while those taking it by the left hand will go to the Fire to be shown their deeds that is the requital for them in either Paradise or the Fire.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:7
body: So whoever does an atom’s weight of good shall see it he shall see its reward

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 99:8
body: and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil shall see it he shall see its requital.
